questions.question: Given matrix A = [[2, 3], [1, 4]] and matrix B = [[1, -1], [2, 0]], find the determinant of the product AB.
Matrix C = [[a, 2], [3, 5]] has a determinant of 11. Find the value of a.
questions.explanation: The determinant is 5a - 6 = 11. Solve: 5a = 17, so a = 17/5 = 3.4. (Correction: det = a(5) - 2(3) = 5a - 6 = 11 gives 5a = 17, thus a = 17/5.)

questions.question: Two matrices P and Q are 2×2. If det(P) = -3 and det(Q) = 4, what is det(P²Q)?
questions.explanation: Use the property det(XY) = det(X)·det(Y). Then det(P²Q) = det(P²)·det(Q) = [det(P)]²·det(Q) = (-3)²·4 = 9·4 = 36.

questions.question: Matrix M = [[1, 2, 0], [0, 3, 1], [2, 0, 4]] is a 3×3 matrix. Calculate det(M) by expanding along the first row.
If matrix A = [[2, 5], [1, 3]], find A⁻¹ and verify that A·A⁻¹ = I.
A system of linear equations is represented as AX = B, where A = [[2, -1], [3, 1]] and B = [[5], [11]]. Use the inverse matrix method to find X = [[x], [y]]. What is x + y?
